commit.message:
Fix window close hanging on some PCs

Move disk I/O out of OnClosing so the window disappears instantly.
Window bounds are captured in-memory during OnClosing, then persisted
in ShutdownRequested via Task.Run (avoids UI-thread deadlock when
_writeLock is held by an in-flight save). Both SaveAsync and
CopilotService.DisposeAsync now have timeouts to prevent infinite hangs.

commit.message:
Fix perf regression: limit debug info to relevant subprograms (#8174) (#8177)

PR #7799 added debug info for global variables across all inlined
subprograms, creating O(subprograms x globals) debug instructions. This
caused 2-3x slower compilation for shaders with many globals and
deeply-inlined functions.

Fix: Instead of collecting ALL subprograms for a function (walking every
instruction), only collect subprograms from the alloca's actual users --
the instructions that reference the specific global variable. This
preserves the debug info feature (globals visible in inlined scopes that
use them) while eliminating work for unrelated scopes.

The 'Lower static global into Alloca' pass is 3.8x faster on a stress
test (41ms -> 11ms), and overall debug compilation is ~10% faster.

Fixes #8174

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Split NuGet mega-packages to stay under 500MB feed limit (#3584)

* Split NuGet mega-packages to stay under 500MB feed limit

The _NuGets, _NuGetsPreview, _Symbols, and _SymbolsPreview packages
exceeded the Azure DevOps 500MB size limit. Split each into:
  - .Managed (managed-only nupkgs, small)
  - .NativeAssets (native asset nupkgs, large)
  - Parent meta-package (depends on both sub-packages)

The download side (DownloadPackageAsync) already follows dependencies,
so consumers automatically get both sub-packages when requesting the
parent.

New nuspec templates:
  _NuGets.Managed.nuspec, _NuGets.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _NuGetsPreview.Managed.nuspec, _NuGetsPreview.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _Symbols.Managed.nuspec, _Symbols.NativeAssets.nuspec
  _SymbolsPreview.Managed.nuspec, _SymbolsPreview.NativeAssets.nuspec

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace Managed/NativeAssets split with dynamic bin-packing

Instead of manually splitting _NuGets into .Managed and .NativeAssets
sub-packages, dynamically bin-pack all nupkgs into ~200 MB numbered
chunks (_NuGets.Dependencies.1, .2, .3, etc.). The parent meta-package
depends on all chunks.

This is self-balancing: as packages grow or shrink, the chunk count
adjusts automatically without any manual curation.

- Delete 8 .Managed/.NativeAssets nuspec files
- Add generic _Dependencies.nuspec template for chunks
- Rewrite nuget-special Cake target with first-fit decreasing bin-packing
- _NativeAssets handling (raw native binaries) is unchanged

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[ResponseOps][Alerting] Alerting v2 (#247464) ## Summary ### Key capabilities - **ES|QL-native rule evaluation** — Rules are defined as ES|QL queries with optional WHERE clause conditions, evaluated on a configurable schedule - **Alert lifecycle management** — Full episode tracking with pending → active → recovering → inactive state transitions, including configurable alert delay (consecutive breaches / duration) - **Event-driven architecture** — Alert events and actions are stored in dedicated data streams (`.alerting-events`, `.alerting-actions`) with ES|QL views for querying - **Notification dispatch pipeline** — A multi-step dispatcher that matches alert episodes to notification policies, handles throttling/suppression, and triggers Kibana Workflows using encrypted API keys - **Notification policies** — CRUD APIs and UI for creating notification policies with KQL-based rule matching, workflow integration, and API key management - **Rule authoring UI** — A shared rule form package (`@kbn/alerting-v2-rule-form`) usable standalone or embedded in Discover, with ES|QL editor, WHERE clause condition editing, recovery configuration, and live query preview - **Rule management UI** — Full rule list with pagination, enable/disable, clone, edit, and delete operations - **APM instrumentation** — Middleware and decorators for tracing rule execution and client operations ### Architecture highlights - **InversifyJS DI** — All services use constructor injection with typed tokens, scoped per-request or singleton as appropriate - **Pipeline pattern** — Rule executor and dispatcher use composable step-based pipelines - **Saved Objects** — Rules stored as hidden saved objects; notification policies stored as encrypted saved objects (for API key protection) - **Feature privileges** — Dedicated Kibana feature with read/all privileges for RBAC --- ## Contained PRs
Core Engine & Plugin Init (12 PRs) - #247283 — Init alerting v2 plugin (@cnasikas) - #247452 — Add the alerting v2 feature privileges (@cnasikas) - #247673 — Director (@cnasikas) - #248306 — Create basic services (@cnasikas) - #248696 — Initialize all resources (@cnasikas) - #250023 — Schema package (@cnasikas) - #250010 — YML Editor (@cnasikas) - #251064 — Remove index.mode: lookup for RnA alert indices (@cnasikas) - #251707 — Simplify task registration pattern (@kdelemme) - #251876 — Dedicated user service (@cnasikas) - #252073 — Use `kbn/data-streams` in alerting_v2 (@cnasikas) - #255120 — Update alerting-v2 owner to new rna project team (@cnasikas)
Rule Execution Pipeline (12 PRs) - #247472 — Add alerting v2 Rule Executor (@darnautov) - #248285 — Alerting v2 rule HTTP APIs (@darnautov) - #248728 — Add basic alert actions route (@darnautov) - #250161 — Refactor rule executor to use a pipeline pattern (@darnautov) - #252292 — Implement the CountTimeframeStrategy for the director (@cnasikas) - #252544 — Add support of streaming in the rule executor (@darnautov) - #252754 — Update rule attributes (@kdelemme) - #253355 — Add getRules client method (@kdelemme) - #253668 — Make evaluation.query.condition optional (@kdelemme) - #254031 — Add recovery event generation to rule execution pipeline (@kdelemme) - #255968 — ES|QL views (@adcoelho) - #256697 — Create episodes ES|QL view (@adcoelho)
Alert Suppression & Episodes (3 PRs) - #252174 — Alert suppression (@kdelemme) - #256486 — Fix suppression query (@kdelemme) - #256527 — Store 'unmatched' action for unmatched alert episodes (@kdelemme)
Dispatcher & Notification Engine (6 PRs) - #250822 — Alerting v2 dispatcher (@kdelemme) - #251529 — Use query service in dispatcher (@kdelemme) - #251679 — Dispatcher task (@kdelemme) - #252758 — Dispatcher notification policy (@kdelemme) - #255332 — Wait for resources before scheduling dispatcher task (@kdelemme) - #256536 — Use stored encrypted API keys from Notification Policy in dispatcher step (@kdelemme)
Notification Policies (Server) (4 PRs) - #251336 — Introduce notification policy CRUD APIs and client (@cnasikas) - #253134 — Update notification policy (@cnasikas) - #254808 — Store API key owner on Notification Policy (@kdelemme) - #256940 — Make notification policies global with optional rule-label scoping (@kdelemme)

Rule Authoring UI (13 PRs) - #250961 — Add create rule flyout in Discover (@adcoelho) - #255111 — Add activation configuration fields to alerting V2 rule form (@yiannisnikolopoulos) - #255427 — Rule form: provide services via context (@dominiqueclarke) - #255876 — MVP rule form, Split evaluation condition, and Recovery configuration (@dominiqueclarke) - #256260 — Foundational rule list (@dominiqueclarke) - #256756 — Wire up edit flow (@dominiqueclarke) - #256801 — Move consecutive breaches max to shared constants (@yiannisnikolopoulos) - #256818 — Preview query and design parity (@dominiqueclarke) - #256938 — Allow clearing number inputs in state transition fields (@yiannisnikolopoulos) - #257017 — Add enable/disable and clone rule to rule list (@dominiqueclarke) - #257246 — Remove all React.FC (@dominiqueclarke) - #257415 — Rule form - fix test (@dominiqueclarke) - #257454 — Block comma key in number input component (@yiannisnikolopoulos)
API Documentation & Schema (2 PRs) - #254901 — Rename indexes for alert events and actions (@adcoelho) - #255810 — OAS for alert action routes (@adcoelho)

* Merge speckit.implement changes into copilot/bug-bash-code-review-fixes

* Initial plan

* fix(security): sanitize X-Request-ID header, add SQL column whitelist, fix truncateFilename edge case

Bug 1 - Request ID header injection: Client-provided X-Request-ID was
reflected into response headers without validation. Malicious values with
CRLF or special characters could potentially be used for header injection.
Fixed by adding a regex whitelist that only allows alphanumeric chars,
hyphens, underscores, and dots (max 128 chars). Invalid values fall back
to a generated uuid4 hex.

Bug 2 - Missing SQL column whitelist in app_service.py: The update_app()
function built dynamic SQL SET clauses from dictionary keys without
validating them against an allowlist. Other services (chores, pipelines)
already implement this defense-in-depth pattern. Added _APP_UPDATABLE_COLUMNS
frozenset and validation check consistent with the existing codebase pattern.

Bug 3 - truncateFilename negative slice: When a filename had an extension
longer than the max parameter, the base slice index became negative,
producing unexpected truncation. Fixed with Math.max(1, ...) to ensure
at least 1 character of the base name is preserved.

All fixes include regression tests. All existing tests continue to pass.

commit.message:
fix: add /claims endpoint and fix wizard Thymeleaf expression

Two bugs fixed:

1. Missing GET /debts/claims endpoint (debt-service)
   The creditor portal called /debt-service/api/v1/debts/claims for all
   claim list views (recovery, hearing, zero-balance, rejected). The endpoint
   did not exist; Spring routed 'claims' to @GetMapping(/{id}), causing
   Invalid UUID string: claims and opening the circuit breaker.

   Added:
   - CreditorClaimListItemDto: superset of ClaimListItemDto and
     HearingClaimListItemDto so both list views deserialize correctly
   - DebtRepository.findClaimsForCreditor(): filters by creditorOrgId,
     lifecycleState (mapped from status param), and zero-balance flag
   - DebtService.getClaimsForCreditor() interface + DebtServiceImpl impl
   - DebtController: GET /claims before /{id} to avoid ambiguity

   Status mapping: IN_RECOVERY->OVERDRAGET, HEARING->HOERING,
   REJECTED->TILBAGEKALDT, ZERO_BALANCE->outstandingBalance=0

2. Thymeleaf 500 on opret fordring wizard (creditor-portal)
   The step indicator in all four wizard templates used 'step < currentStep'
   in a th:classappend expression. The < character is invalid in HTML attribute
   values and caused a TemplateProcessingException. Fixed by replacing < with
   the Thymeleaf comparison alias 'lt' in all four step-*.html templates.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Redesign Gallery as interactive Blazor WASM showcase with 21 demos (#3578)

Replace 44 thin static samples with 21 rich interactive demos, each with
live controls (sliders, pickers, toggles, composable effect groups).

Project structure:
- Shared/ as net10.0 class library, Blazor/ as WASM host
- Solution converted to .slnx format
- Removed all legacy gallery platforms (UWP, WPF, Xamarin.Forms, etc.)

Architecture:
- SampleBase → CanvasSampleBase + DocumentSampleBase
- SliderControl/ToggleControl/PickerControl/GroupControl records
- DI-based SampleService with reflection discovery
- TreatWarningsAsErrors enabled, zero Gallery warnings

Demos: 2D/3D Transforms, Blend Modes, Blur, GIF Player, Gradient,
Image Decoder, Lottie Player, Nine-Patch, Noise Generator, Path Builder,
Path Effects Lab, Photo Lab, Shader Playground (5 SkSL presets),
Text Lab, Text on Path, Vector Art, Vertex Mesh, World Text,

commit.message:
fix: add missing /counts endpoint to DebtController

The creditor portal called GET /debt-service/api/v1/debts/counts
but the endpoint did not exist. Spring routed the request to
@GetMapping(/{id}) treating 'counts' as a UUID path variable,
causing MethodArgumentTypeMismatchException. This opened the
circuit breaker in the portal, showing zero counts on the dashboard.

Added:
- ClaimCountsDto in debt-service with inRecovery/inHearing/rejected/zeroBalance
- DebtRepository: countByCreditorAndLifecycleState + countZeroBalanceByCreditor
- DebtService.getClaimCounts(UUID) interface method + implementation
- DebtController: @GetMapping('/counts') declared before /{id} to avoid ambiguity

Also fixed start-demo.ps1: corrected the 'Opret fordring' URL from
/fordring/ny to /fordring/opret.

commit.message:
Complete Metal.jl GPU acceleration

- Fix run_gpu! loop to follow exact 16-step CPU integration order
- Fix recovery duration: use lognormal (matching CPU default) instead of exponential
- Fix dynamic network support: re-generate edges each step (was using static cached edges)
- Add SEIR GPU support (exposed→infected transition, recovery time setting)
- Fix SIS GPU: use ti_recovered-based recovery, add immunity waning + boost
- Add cache_edges option for static network optimization
- Optimize results: GPU-side sum() instead of full state download
- GPUDiseaseArrays extended with exposed/ti_exposed fields
- Add _sample_recovery_durations helper matching CPU distribution
- Correctness verified: r>0.999 for SIR, SIS, SEIR (30 seeds)
- Update GPU vignette with actual benchmarks and working examples
- Extension now 1,696 lines (was 1,385)

commit.message:
fix(person-registry): use JdbcTemplate in seeders to bypass Hibernate state detection

Replace JPA repository.save() with JdbcTemplate INSERT ... ON CONFLICT to avoid
Hibernate 6.x PropertyValueException when persisting entities with explicitly-set
UUIDs and @Version/@GeneratedValue annotations.

Also adds JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS to payment-service to disable grpc-netty native epoll
transport, resolving the SIGSEGV (exit code 139) on container startup.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
